description_long: This is a life-sized stone sculpture of a robed female figure, possibly a saint, standing upright on a rectangular plinth. The figure wears a long, flowing garment with detailed folds and holds a palm frond in her left arm, a symbol often associated with martyrdom. Her right hand is raised, holding a small object, and she wears a serene expression. A thin, circular metallic halo with a star at the top is affixed behind her head, suggesting religious significance. The surface of the sculpture is weathered, with visible patina and discoloration, indicating age and exposure to the elements. The back of the sculpture shows the drapery cascading down, and the base is solid and rectangular.
description: A weathered stone sculpture of a robed female figure with a metallic halo, holding a palm frond and standing on a rectangular base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed face, raised right hand, palm frond in the left arm, and the full halo above the head.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the face, the depth of the drapery, and the protruding halo.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flowing drapery down the back and the full circular halo, with no facial features visible.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the profile of the face, the palm frond, and the layered garment folds.
attribute_subject_matter: robed female figure with halo and palm frond
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
